body:
Closes #686.

## What & why

Issue #686 asks the project to keep a **persistent version of meta-language expressions saved in an associative links network**, to **count usages (reads) and changes (writes)** so the most used or changed knowledge persists longer, to **derive usage from incoming and outgoing links**, and to **keep everything as a link / link network** (not graph, not edges, not vertices) вЂ” applying the best practices from the [Wikontic paper](https://huggingface.co/papers/2512.00590).

This PR delivers that as a new library module plus the required deep case study.

## Approach вЂ” audit and wire

The stack already had the substrate: a links network (`SubstitutionGraph`), content addressing (`stable_id`, so one meaning is one node), and an LFU read-count precursor in `src/dreaming.rs`. The three gaps issue #686 fills are a **write counter**, an **outgoing-link degree** usage signal, and a **first-class store for the expressions themselves**. The new `AssociativeMemory` (`src/associative_persistence.rs`) closes them.

### `AssociativeMemory` (`src/associative_persistence.rs`)
- **Persistence** вЂ” `persist(text)` stores each meta-language expression as a content-addressed node (`stable_id`) in an embedded `SubstitutionGraph`; re-persisting the same meaning increments its write count instead of duplicating.
- **Reads vs. writes** вЂ” every expression carries independent `reads` and `writes` counters (`note_read` / `note_write`); a fresh expression starts at `writes = 1, reads = 0`.
- **Link-derived usage** вЂ” `in_degree` / `out_degree` / `degree` compute usage purely from a node's incoming and outgoing associative links.
- **Retention** вЂ” `retention_score = readВ·reads + writeВ·writes + incomingВ·in_degree + outgoingВ·out_degree` (configurable `RetentionWeights`, saturating arithmetic). `retain_most_used` / `evict_least_used` / `eviction_order` implement the LFU-style policy so the most used, most changed, most connected knowledge is the last to go; `forget` removes an expression together with its incident links.
- **Links-only & deterministic** вЂ” everything is a link; `links_notation()` serializes to Links Notation, `from_context` ingests an issue #649 world-model `Context` preserving statement ids and dependency links, and the whole policy avoids clocks/randomness (sorted, reproducible output).

## Case study (`docs/case-studies/issue-686/`)

Mirrors the established issue-649 pattern: `README.md` (deep analysis), `requirements.md` (R686-01вЂ¦R686-13), `persistence-mapping.md` (concept в†’ associative-stack status), `solution-plans.md` (per-requirement plans + prior-art survey), and `raw-data/` with cited `online-research.md`. Online research covers the Wikontic paper's entity-degreeв†”retrieval and alias-dedup lessons, AriGraph, LFU/LRU cache replacement, reference counting, and degree centrality.

Requirement rows **R445вЂ“R452** are added to `REQUIREMENTS.md`; `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, and a `changelog.d` fragment are wired accordingly.

## How to verify / tests

- `tests/unit/issue_686_associative_persistence.rs` вЂ” 10 unit tests: content-addressed deterministic persistence, independent read/write counting, links-network degree, combined retention score, most-used-persists-longest eviction, incident-link forgetting, deterministic tie-breaks, `from_context` ingestion, and reproducible Links Notation.
- `tests/unit/docs_requirements_issue_686.rs` вЂ” traceability test asserting every case-study document, REQUIREMENTS rows, and raw-data capture is present.

```
cargo test --test unit 686        # 11 passed
cargo fmt --check                 # clean
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features   # clean under RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings
```

Full `cargo test` passes except one pre-existing flaky HTTP integration test (`issue_558_self_healing::вЂ¦repair_case_write`, a socket `WouldBlock` under parallel load) that passes in isolation and is unrelated to these library-only changes.
title: Associative knowledge networks learning: usage-weighted persistence (#686)
